jobs.prompt:
Delivery rule: scheduled assistant output is not auto-forwarded. If there is useful output, send it to Telegram chat `507960755` with `telegram_send_message`. If there is no material update or only healthy/no-news status, return exactly `[SILENT]` and do not send Telegram.

Check Tony's ADU permit status for the project at 122 Walnut St, Menlo Park, CA 94025.

Behavior:
- This is a quiet change detector. If there is no material change, respond exactly `[SILENT]`.
- Material changes: overall status changed, new Resubmittal Received event, a review newly active/completed, new reviewer comment, changed condition/notice, or a new document/comment after the last known corrections decision.
- Current known baseline: overall `Pending Resubmittal`; Review Decision completed `04/22/2026 by GDO, as Corrections`; PW Engineering / Flood completed `04/22/2026 by EMH, as Corrections`; Building Review completed `04/15/2026 by 3WC, as Corrections`; Detailed Planning completed `04/22/2026 by crt, as Corrections`; next expected change is a new resubmittal that flips status back to `In Review`.

If there is a material change, write a concise Chinese Telegram update:
- First line: `**ADU Permit Update** — <main status/change>`
- 2-4 short bullets max.
- Include the Accela record number `BLD2025-01110` and any changed date/comment.
- Use Markdown links with descriptive text if including URLs.

Do not send Telegram yourself; final response is auto-delivered.

Delivery rule: scheduled assistant output is not auto-forwarded. If there is useful output, send it to Telegram chat `507960755` with `telegram_send_message`. If there is no material update or only healthy/no-news status, return exactly `[SILENT]` and do not send Telegram.

Prepare Tony's morning briefing in Chinese. Deliver it as a single Telegram message.

## Mandatory data collection

- Calendar: check today's agenda.
- News: collect 2-4 major international headlines.
- **Tech / Hacker News: mandatory.** Fetch Hacker News front page via Algolia:
  `https://hn.algolia.com/api/v1/search?tags=front_page&hitsPerPage=60`
  Then rank all 60 items by Tony's interests: AI/ML, Apple, GitHub/open-source, dev tools, coding, design, infrastructure. Points are only a tiebreaker. Select 3-5 high-signal items. Do not invent items. If the API fails, put one short failure line in Tech.
- X: **must use Agent Reach Twitter/X cookie channel**, not `xurl` or official X API. Verify/read with:
  `twitter status --json`
  `twitter feed -t following -n 50 --json`
  If Following has fewer than 2 strong posts, optionally fetch:
  `twitter feed -t for-you -n 50 --json`
  Keep 2-4 high-signal posts only; if nothing useful, write `无`. Prefer Following over For You; filter out ads, 擦边, political ragebait, traffic alerts, and low-quality repost chains.

## Output contract

Title line, then sections in this exact order (today-focused):

1. 📅 `**Daily Brief — YYYY-MM-DD (周X) · 早报**` — bold title line, one line only
2. ⚡ **Priorities** — top 3 things that matter today, ranked. Each bullet MUST start with an action verb + timebox (e.g., `上午`, `午前`, `今日内`). One line per item.
3. 🧭 **What's next** — proactive plan for Tony: 3-5 concrete next moves across today / this week / can-delegate. Use short bullets like `今天：...`, `本周：...`, `可委托：...`. Connect calendar, signals, current projects, LinkedIn/X posting opportunities, reimbursements, and deadlines when useful. Include a concrete LinkedIn or X writing suggestion when there is a credible prompt, e.g. `LinkedIn：<hook>` or `X：<one-line take>`. Do not write generic personal-brand advice or duplicate Priorities verbatim.
4. 📆 **Today** — calendar agenda as `HH:MM — event` bullets; flag conflicts or gaps with ⚠️. Call out deep-work windows.
5. 🧾 **Tasks** — ☑️ must-do today / ▫️ later. Do NOT repeat Priorities verbatim.
6. 📰 **News** — 2-4 major international / macro / non-work headlines. One line each: `headline — takeaway`. Chinese-first phrasing, English only for proper nouns.
7. 💻 **Tech** — Hacker News sourced items, 3-5 bullets, each as `• [Chinese headline](url) · {points}分 — one-line takeaway`. This section is required even if News already contains tech-adjacent macro items.
8. 🐦 **X** — 2-4 high-signal posts as `[@handle](link): one-line point`, or `无`.
9. 💡 **Note** — one insight or nudge for the day.

**NO email section.** Email is now a separate independent briefing.

## Deduplication rule

Each piece of information appears in ONE section only. If an item is in Priorities, it should NOT repeat in Tasks with the same detail — reference it briefly or omit. News is for world/macro; Tech is for HN items. If the same item appears in both feeds, keep it in Tech unless it is a major macro/geopolitical headline.

## Formatting rules

- Separate major sections with a blank line. No `---` dividers.
- Section headers use the emoji + `**bold**` label pattern shown above.
- Keep bullets to one idea each; no paragraphs, no tables.
- `**bold**` only for the single most important fact per bullet.
- Dates / amounts / IDs / names in `inline code` when it aids scanning.
- URLs as `[descriptive text](url)` — no naked URLs.
- Chinese-first; English only for proper nouns and technical terms.
- If a non-Tech section has nothing worth reporting, write one line: `无`. Never drop the section.
- No preamble, no footer, no "Cronjob Response" wrapper text.

## Self-check before sending

- All 9 sections present, in order, each with its emoji
- Priorities bullets all start with action verb + timebox
- What's next has 3-5 concrete proactive next moves and no generic advice
- News section has 2-4 international headlines
- Tech section is present and is sourced from HN Algolia front page
- X section is either 2-4 posts from `twitter feed` (Agent Reach cookie channel) or `无`
- No item appears in two sections with the same detail
- Links are clickable markdown links
- Message scans cleanly on mobile

Delivery rule: scheduled assistant output is not auto-forwarded. If there is useful output, send it to Telegram chat `507960755` with `telegram_send_message`. If there is no material update or only healthy/no-news status, return exactly `[SILENT]` and do not send Telegram.

Prepare Tony's evening briefing in Chinese. Deliver it as a single Telegram message.

## Mandatory data collection

- Calendar: check tomorrow's agenda.
- News: collect 2-4 major international headlines.
- **Tech / Hacker News: mandatory.** Fetch Hacker News front page via Algolia:
  `https://hn.algolia.com/api/v1/search?tags=front_page&hitsPerPage=60`

## Output contract

Title line, then sections in this exact order (tomorrow-focused; today is already covered by morning/daytime briefings):

1. 📅 `**Daily Brief — YYYY-MM-DD (周X) · 晚报**` — bold title line, one line only
2. ⚡ **Priorities** — top 3 unfinished / carry-over items, ranked. Each bullet MUST start with an action verb + deadline/timebox (e.g., `今晚24h内` / `明早` / `本周`). One line per item.
3. 🧭 **What's next** — proactive plan for Tony: 3-5 concrete next moves across tonight / tomorrow / this week / can-delegate. Use short bullets like `今晚：...`, `明天：...`, `本周：...`, `可委托：...`. Connect calendar, signals, current projects, LinkedIn/X posting opportunities, reimbursements, and deadlines when useful. Include a concrete LinkedIn or X writing suggestion when there is a credible prompt, e.g. `LinkedIn：<hook>` or `X：<one-line take>`. Do not write generic personal-brand advice or duplicate Priorities verbatim.
4. 📆 **Tomorrow** — tomorrow's calendar as `HH:MM — event` bullets. Call out deep-work windows or conflicts. Do NOT recap today's meetings.
5. 🧾 **Tasks** — ☑️ must-do tomorrow / ▫️ later. Do NOT repeat Priorities verbatim; reference them briefly if needed.
6. 📰 **News** — 2-4 major international / macro / non-work headlines. One line each: `headline — takeaway`. Chinese-first phrasing, English only for proper nouns.
7. 💻 **Tech** — Hacker News sourced items, 3-5 bullets, each as `• [Chinese headline](url) · {points}分 — one-line takeaway`. This section is required even if News already contains tech-adjacent macro items.
8. 🐦 **X** — 2-4 high-signal posts as `[@handle](link): one-line point`, or `无`.
9. 💡 **Note** — one reflection or prep nudge for tomorrow.

**NO email section.** Email is now a separate independent briefing.

## Deduplication rule

Each piece of information appears in ONE section only. News is for world/macro; Tech is for HN items. If the same item appears in both feeds, keep it in Tech unless it is a major macro/geopolitical headline.

## Formatting rules

- Separate major sections with a blank line. No `---` dividers.
- Section headers use the emoji + `**bold**` label pattern shown above.
- Keep bullets to one idea each; no paragraphs, no tables.
- `**bold**` only for the single most important fact per bullet.
- Dates / amounts / IDs / names in `inline code` when it aids scanning.
- URLs as `[descriptive text](url)` — no naked URLs.
- Chinese-first; English only for proper nouns and technical terms.
- If a non-Tech section has nothing worth reporting, write one line: `无`. Never drop the section.
- No preamble, no footer, no "Cronjob Response" wrapper text.

## Cross-briefing deduplication

Before finalizing, use `session_search` to find today's earlier briefing(s). Aggressively remove any item (news, signal, X post) already reported today UNLESS there is a material update. Do NOT remove the Tech section entirely; if all HN items were already reported, fetch current HN and pick the next 3 relevant items or state `无新高信号`.

## Self-check before sending

- All 9 sections present, in order, each with its emoji
- Priorities bullets all start with action verb + deadline
- What's next has 3-5 concrete proactive next moves and no generic advice
- Today's meeting recap is NOT in the briefing (晚报只看明天)
- News section has 2-4 international headlines
- Tech section is present and is sourced from HN Algolia front page
- X section is either 2-4 posts from `twitter feed` (Agent Reach cookie channel) or `无`
- No item appears in two sections with the same detail
- Links are clickable markdown links
- No item duplicated from an earlier briefing today unless materially updated
- Message scans cleanly on mobile

Delivery rule: scheduled assistant output is not auto-forwarded. If there is useful output, send it to Telegram chat `507960755` with `telegram_send_message`. If there is no material update or only healthy/no-news status, return exactly `[SILENT]` and do not send Telegram.

Prepare Tony's morning email briefing in Chinese. Deliver as a single Telegram message.

## 数据收集

Use gws CLI at `/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin/gws`.

1. Broad triage (48h, exclude noise):
```bash
/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin/gws gmail +triage --max 30 --query "is:unread newer_than:2d -category:promotions -category:social -category:forums" --format json
```

2. Important senders pass:
```bash
/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin/gws gmail +triage --max 20 --query "(TurboTax OR Intuit OR United OR Bitwarden OR Fidelity OR Synology OR State Farm OR PenFed OR USPS OR Chase OR Citi OR Amazon) newer_than:2d" --format json
```

3. Deep-read important messages with `+read --id MESSAGE_ID --format json`.
4. If Recruiting-labelled or recruiter/hiring emails appear in the triage window, include them with a suggested decline draft instead of saying they are ignored.

## 输出格式

📬 **邮件简报 — YYYY-MM-DD 早**

按优先级列出，每条一行：
- 🔥 紧急（安全/账户/支付告警）
- 📎 重要（财务/物流/行政）
- 💬 需回复 — 附草稿回复：`💬 草稿：「内容」`

**草稿规则：**
- 加载 tony-email-voice skill，严格遵循 Tony 写作风格
- 简单直接的才起草；复杂/敏感标记"需 Tony 判断"
- 招聘/猎头邮件必须给建议 draft，默认 Tony 风格短婉拒："Thanks for reaching out. I'm not looking for new opportunities right now, but appreciate you thinking of me."

**过滤规则：**
- 财务类必须包含金额、商户、账户标识
- 物流类包含承运商和预计到达时间
- 同一线程/主题合并为一条
- 零售促销、newsletter、社交摘要直接跳过
- 招聘邮件低优先级，但如果列出就必须附建议拒绝 draft，不能只写“忽略/无需处理”


## Maintenance visibility

Before writing the briefing, read `/home/tonyxu/.local/state/copilot-home-assistant/email_butler_ops_latest.json` if it exists. Include a short `🛠 **Maintenance**` section near the end telling Tony what Email Butler maintenance recently did.

Report only safe, useful counts/status from that file:
- labels: labels maintained and `total_new_labeled` if > 0; otherwise say label maintenance ran with no new labels added.
- action queue: new actionable items count if > 0; otherwise say no new action queue items.
- sync: Gmail/Calendar collectors + gbrain imports status/counts at a high level.
- archive: only mention if `ran_archive` is true or `total_archived` > 0; say it removed `INBOX` only, not deleted.

Keep it to 1-3 bullets. Do not include message IDs, raw JSON, tokens, DB URLs, stack traces, or full email bodies. If the latest ops status is not success, surface it as a warning in this section.

## 格式规则

- 中文为主，英文仅用于专有名词
- **bold** 只给真正重要的
- 不用表格
- 通常 5-10 条，不凑数
- 无事可报就写一行：`📬 邮件简报 — 无新邮件`
- No preamble, no footer

Delivery rule: scheduled assistant output is not auto-forwarded. If there is useful output, send it to Telegram chat `507960755` with `telegram_send_message`. If there is no material update or only healthy/no-news status, return exactly `[SILENT]` and do not send Telegram.

Tony wants a daily editorial workflow that can turn selected fragmented thoughts, saved links, notes, and personal activity signals into public-safe X/Twitter and LinkedIn draft options.

Use these skills and follow their current instructions: x-twitter-workflows, linkedin-workflows, memory, gbrain-operations, limemo-capture.

Canonical repo: /home/tonyxu/brain

Rules:
- Brain repo is the canonical human-readable source; GBrain is the searchable/indexed layer.
- memo = general personal memo, not a content inbox. Raw personal memo path: /home/tonyxu/brain/notes/memo/YYYY-MM-DD.md.
- limemo = work memo. Raw work memo path: /home/tonyxu/brain/notes/work/memo/YYYY-MM-DD.md.
- Preserve raw fragments before interpretation. Then route obvious durable items into structured notes under people/, projects/, notes/, or notes/work/.
- Writing lives under /home/tonyxu/brain/notes/writing/, not notes/knowledge/writing/.
- All collected likes/favorites/saves must first be represented in /home/tonyxu/brain/daily/activity/YYYY/YYYY-MM-DD.md as the cross-source day ledger. Date-shard by the content generation date when available, not export date. Examples: X tweet createdAtISO/createdAtLocal, YouTube release/published timestamp, Xiaohongshu item publish/create timestamp, Douban added_at when it is the saved/rated activity timestamp, Google Maps list_added_at_utc. Use export/collection date only as an explicit labeled fallback: date_basis: collection_date.
- Keep source exports under sources/... as provenance. Do not replace source provenance with synthesized notes.
- Activity signals are inspiration/ranking evidence, not Tony-authored claims or automatic endorsements.
- Optional synthesis path: /home/tonyxu/brain/notes/writing/activity-inspiration/YYYY-MM-DD.md.
- Draft output path: /home/tonyxu/brain/notes/writing/content-drafts/YYYY-MM-DD.md.
- Draft only. Do not auto-publish to X or LinkedIn.
- Do not read/import notes/records/private/, credentials, cookies, tokens, API keys, or company internal Slack/comms.
- For company-sensitive content, only use work memo Tony explicitly provided, and keep public drafts high-level/public-safe.

Daily workflow:
1. Read recent raw memo/limemo and structured notes from the last 24-36h.
2. Check daily/activity for recent activity ledger coverage. If recent collected source exports exist but the corresponding daily activity file is missing, create/update the daily activity file using the content generation date rule above before synthesis.
3. Build or update activity-inspiration/YYYY-MM-DD.md only for high-signal recurring themes.
4. Produce a compact content-drafts/YYYY-MM-DD.md with selected public-safe X/LinkedIn draft options, linked back to source notes/activity.
5. Sync touched Markdown pages to GBrain narrowly with gbrain put <slug> < file.md then gbrain embed <slug>. Use gbrain embed --stale only for batch reconciliation or if stats show Embedded < Chunks.
6. Deliver a Telegram message only if there are useful drafts, errors, warnings, or blockers. No routine success noise.

Canonical contract:
- Brain repo `/home/tonyxu/brain` is the human-readable source of truth.

2. Run `gbrain stats`. If `Embedded < Chunks`, run `gbrain embed --stale` once, then run `gbrain stats` again. Report only if it still fails or drift remains material.
3. Run `python3 scripts/verify_daily_activity_coverage.py --json`. Report if it fails, if expected labels are missing, or if there are new severe warnings. Known non-blocking warning: existing zero-byte raw artifacts may be reported; do not delete them.
4. Inspect `/home/tonyxu/brain/state/activity_collector_state.json` and `/home/tonyxu/.local/state/copilot-home-assistant/email_butler_ops_latest.json` if present. Report only stale/failing collector state, not routine success.
5. Check `notes/unsorted/_promotion_queue.md` age/size lightly. If it is growing or has old unresolved items, report a concise backlog recommendation; do not auto-edit it.
6. Check git status for `/home/tonyxu/brain`. Report if there are uncommitted changes that appear sensitive or inexplicable. Generated collector/source updates are okay but should be mentioned only if unusually large.

Output contract:
- If all checks are healthy or only routine generated changes exist: `[SILENT]`
- If issues exist: concise Chinese Telegram message, first line `**Second Brain Health** — <main issue>`, then max 5 bullets with exact failing command/state and recommended action.

- Do not schedule other cron jobs.
